title: Helenium arbuscula
intro: Helenium arbuscula, also known as Helenium, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to North America. Helenium has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Helenium include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Helenium is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also added to topical creams and salves for its potential skin-soothing and wound-healing effects.
taxonomy: Helenium arbuscula is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family and is one of the largest and most diverse plant families. Helenium arbuscula, along with other members of the Asteraceae family, is an annual or perennial flowering plant. The classification of Helenium arbuscula is further divided into the genus Helenium, which consists of about 40 species of flowering plants. These plants are native to North and Central America, and are often found in moist environments.
uses: Helenium arbuscula, also known as shrubby sneezeweed, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains compounds that may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. It has been used topically to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The leaves and flowers of Helenium arbuscula may also be used to make a tea that can help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Further research is needed to fully confirm the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

constituents: Helenium arbuscula, also known as rock false sunflower, is a plant species that contains a variety of active constituents. These active constituents include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their potential medicinal properties.
